2603.29543.title: Reducing Complexity for Quantum Approaches in Train Load Optimization
2603.29543.paper_summary: Efficiently planning container loads onto trains is a computationally challenging combinatorial optimization problem, central to logistics and supply chain management. A primary source of this complexity arises from the need to model and reduce rehandle operations-unproductive crane moves required to access blocked containers. Conventional mathematical formulations address this by introducing explicit binary variables and a web of logical constraints for each potential rehandle, resulting in large-scale models that are difficult to solve. This paper presents a fundamental departure from this paradigm. We introduce an innovative and compact mathematical formulation for the Train Load Optimization (TLO) problem where the rehandle cost is calculated implicitly within the objective function. This novel approach helps prevent the need for dedicated rehandle variables and their associated constraints, leading to a dramatic reduction in model size. We provide a formal comparison against a conventional model to analytically demonstrate the significant reduction in the number of variables and constraints. The efficacy of our compact formulation is assessed through a simulated annealing metaheuristic, which finds high-quality loading plans for various problem instances. The results confirm that our model is not only more parsimonious but also practically effective, offering a scalable and powerful tool for modern rail logistics.
2603.29543.paper_summary_zh: 有效地規劃貨櫃裝載到火車上是一個計算上具有挑戰性的組合優化問題，對於物流和供應鏈管理至關重要。<lb/>這種複雜性的主要來源在於需要對重新處理操作進行建模和減少——這是為了訪問被阻塞的貨櫃而需要的無生產力的起重機移動。<lb/>傳統的數學公式通過為每個潛在的重新處理引入明確的二元變數和一系列邏輯約束來解決這個問題，導致難以解決的大規模模型。<lb/>本文提出了一種根本性的偏離這一範式的方法。<lb/>我們為火車裝載優化（TLO）問題引入了一種創新且緊湊的數學公式，其中重新處理成本在目標函數中隱式計算。<lb/>這種新穎的方法有助於避免專用的重新處理變數及其相關約束的需求，從而大幅減少模型的大小。<lb/>我們提供了與傳統模型的正式比較，以分析性地展示變數和約束數量的顯著減少。<lb/>我們的緊湊公式的有效性通過模擬退火元啟發式進行評估，該方法為各種問題實例找到高質量的裝載計劃。<lb/>結果證實，我們的模型不僅更為簡約，而且在實際應用中也有效，為現代鐵路物流提供了一個可擴展且強大的工具。
2603.29543.author: Zhijie Tang et.al.
2603.29543.authors: Zhijie Tang, Albert Nieto-Morales, Arit Kumar Bishwas
